claims.statement: CLOSURE_RECEIPT_V1 schema exists at helen_os/schemas/closure_receipt_v1.json with the canonical contents that this receipt conforms to.
Schema Authority Guard test exists at helen_os/tests/test_schema_authority_guard.py and encodes the measurement conditions for Seam 1 closure.
Legacy schema purge migration script exists at scripts/purge_legacy_schemas.sh. Safe-by-default: dry-run unless --execute; refuses to purge if bare SchemaRegistry() callers remain in helen_os/.

Ghost Closure Detector test exists at helen_os/tests/test_no_ghost_closures.py. Parameterized over GOVERNANCE/CLOSURES/; verifies every claim's artifact exists with the expected SHA-256 and enforces Rule 3 (proposer != attestor).
The legacy root-level schemas/ directory is purged (absent or empty of JSON schemas).
claims.note: Directory still contains 55 legacy schema JSON files. Blocks closure. Remediation: migrate the 2 test callers that rely on bare SchemaRegistry() (helen_os/test_skill_promotion_requires_receipts.py, helen_os/test_failure_bridge_only_accepts_typed_failures.py) to pass schema_dir=Path('helen_os/schemas'), migrate the schemas they need into helen_os/schemas/, then run scripts/purge_legacy_schemas.sh --execute.

note: V4 materializes the MEASUREMENT INSTRUMENTS for Seam 1. V1/V2/V3 were ghost closures — claimed CLOSED without backing artifacts, retracted by direct filesystem inspection. This V4 is the first honest receipt: it proves the instruments exist (C1–C4) and names the specific blocker (C5) that must clear before a V5 CLOSED receipt can issue. The seam is not closed; the closure process is now auditable.
